section_header: tables presenting measures in the transition areas

parent.policy_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
parent.verbatim_text:
This section summarises a large number of measures, with councils, committees and executive boards designated to assume responsibility for helping to achieve the climate goals by 2030.
The following tables list approximately 150 measures divided into the five transition areas. ... New measures will be gradually generated within the five transition areas.
parent.section_header: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas
parent.extraction_rationale: Introduces a named, structured set of measures with responsible entities and ongoing development; serves as an umbrella for many specific sub-measures.

section_header: leading the world in a just climate transition

individual.policy_statement: Adopt and implement Climate Action Plan 2030 as a City-wide policy document with approximately 150 measures across five transition areas to achieve the Environment Programme 2030 climate goals.
individual.verbatim_text: The Climate Action Plan 2030 describes concrete measures to achieve them. This Climate Action Plan includes approximately 150 measures in five areas of transition.
individual.section_header: Leading the world in a just climate transition
individual.extraction_rationale: City-wide plan adoption and implementation framing with defined measure set and scope; constitutes an actionable policy instrument.

individual.policy_statement: Limit emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area to a maximum of 0.6 tonnes CO2e per resident by 2030 and ensure negative emissions are greater than remaining emissions, as part of being climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040.
individual.verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030and fossil-freeby2040
area shall not exceed 0.6 tonnes carbon dioxide equivalents (CO 2 e) per resident. Negative emissions must be greater than the remaining emissions.
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline and defined condition on negative emissions.

individual.policy_statement: Reduce transport-sector emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area by 80% by 2030 compared with 2010.
individual.verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.2 Reduced climate impact from the transport sector
area shall be reduced by 80% by 2030 (compared with 2010).
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with baseline year and deadline.

section_header: climate impact from consumption a different perspective

individual.policy_statement: Halve consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm by 2030 compared with 2019 (from 10.7 to 5.4 tonnes CO2e per resident).
individual.verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.4 Ahalving of emissions from consumption
Consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm shall be halved by 2030 compared with 2019.

According to the City's milestone 2.4, A halving of emissions from consumption, these emissions must be halved to 5.4 tonnes per resident by 2030.
individual.section_header: Climate impact from consumption a different perspective
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified target with baseline and per-capita numeric endpoint.

section_header: emission budget sets the pace in reducing emissions

individual.policy_statement: Establish a local emissions budget for Stockholm for 2024–2040 with a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e emitted over the period to complement Milestone 2.1.
individual.verbatim_text:
This action plan defines a local emissions budget for Stockholm as a complement to milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040 .
Stockholm's emissions budget 2024-2040 states that a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e may be emitted for the period 2024-2040.
individual.section_header: Emission budget sets the pace in reducing emissions
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified carbon budget over a defined period; explicit policy instrument.

individual.policy_statement: Reduce car traffic by 30% by 2030 from the 2017 level.
individual.verbatim_text: By 2030, the City aims to reduce car traffic by 30% from the 2017 level, which requires strong measures.
individual.extraction_rationale: Clear quantified target with baseline year and deadline.

individual.policy_statement: Install and commission a bio-CCS (BECCS) plant in Stockholm to enable capture and permanent storage of 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide annually (commissioning planned for 2027).
individual.verbatim_text:
Building and commissioning a bio-CCS plant in Stockholm will enable 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide to be captured and stored annually.

Negative emissions in the emissions budget assume that a bioCCS/BECCS plant in connection with the biofuel-fired CHP KVV8 at the Värtaverket plant will be built and taken into operation in 2027.
individual.extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure intervention with quantified annual capture and an operational start-year assumption.

individual.policy_statement: Cease all firing using fossil oil by 2025.
individual.verbatim_text: All firing using fossil oil must have ceased by 2025.
individual.extraction_rationale: Clear phase-out requirement with a deadline.

individual.policy_statement: Cease incineration of fossil plastics in district heating by 2040, with the aim to phase it out as soon as possible through increased sorting and recycling of plastics.
individual.verbatim_text: Through increased sorting and recycling of plastics, the aim is to phase out the incineration of fossil plastics in district heating as soon as possible, with the goal of ceasing by 2040.
individual.extraction_rationale: Explicit phase-out goal with an end-date and mechanism direction (sorting/recycling).

individual.policy_statement: Reduce the City’s relative energy use by 10% by 2026 (compared with 2022), as a first step toward more efficient energy use.
individual.verbatim_text: In addition to making the transition to renewable energy, energy needs to be used more efficiently. In a first step, the City aims to reduce its relative energy use by 10% by 2026. 14

section_header: 4. plan, build and develop the city in a circular and sustainable way

individual.policy_statement: Build 140,000 homes with associated community services by 2035 while reducing emissions from the construction and civil engineering sector by 2030 and ultimately achieving climate-neutrality.
individual.verbatim_text: The City aims to build 140,000 homes with associated community services by 2035. At the same time, emissions from the construction and civil engineering sector are significant and need to be reduced by 2030, and eventually to be climate-neutral.
individual.section_header: 4. Plan, build and develop the city in a circular and sustainable way
individual.extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified housing delivery target and a stated emissions-reduction requirement for the sector with a timeframe.
